questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.prompt: In the context of international extradition law, which of the following best describes the 'Principle of Speciality'?

options.text: A person can only be extradited if the act is a crime in both the requesting and the requested states.
A person cannot be extradited if they have already been tried and punished for the same offense.
An extradited person can be prosecuted only for the specific offenses for which their extradition was granted.
A state may refuse extradition if the fugitive is likely to face an unfair trial in the requesting state.

questions.explanation: The Principle of Speciality dictates that the requesting state can only prosecute the extradited individual for the specific crimes for which the extradition was requested and granted. Option A refers to Double Criminality, Option B to Double Jeopardy, and Option D to the Fair Trial principle.
questions.takeaway: The Principle of Speciality is a safeguard that prevents a state from requesting extradition for one crime but prosecuting for another.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Speciality: Limits prosecution to specified offenses in extradition.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.prompt: With reference to the Chemical Weapons Convention (CWC), which of the following features distinguishes it from many other multilateral disarmament agreements?

options.text: It is the only treaty that prohibits the use of weapons of mass destruction during active interstate conflicts.
It is the first agreement to provide for the elimination of an entire category of weapons of mass destruction within a fixed time frame.
It is the only disarmament treaty where the implementing agency has been awarded a Nobel Peace Prize.
It allows for the use of chemical agents for domestic riot control purposes without any oversight from the implementing agency.

questions.explanation: The CWC is uniquely recognized as the world's first multilateral disarmament agreement that provides for the total elimination of an entire category of weapons of mass destruction (chemical weapons) within a specific, fixed time frame. While the OPCW did win the Nobel Peace Prize (2013), other organizations related to disarmament (like ICAN or the IAEA) have also been recognized, making B the most definitive distinguishing feature mentioned in the text.
questions.takeaway: The CWC is unique for its mandate to eliminate an entire class of WMDs within a set timeline.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: It is the World’s first multilateral disarmament agreement to provide for the elimination of an entire category of weapons of mass destruction within a fixed time frame.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.prompt: In the context of international extradition law, the principle of 'Double Criminality' refers to which of the following requirements?

options.text: The fugitive must have committed the same crime in two different countries simultaneously.
The act for which extradition is requested must be a crime in both the requesting and the requested states.
The person cannot be tried for a second time for the same offense once they are extradited.
The requesting state must provide double the evidence required for a local trial to justify extradition.

questions.explanation: Double Criminality is a fundamental principle of extradition which stipulates that the conduct for which extradition is sought must constitute a criminal offense under the laws of both the requesting country and the country where the fugitive is located (the requested state).
questions.takeaway: Double Criminality ensures that a state is not forced to extradite someone for an act that it does not consider a crime.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Double Criminality: Requires the act to be a crime in both jurisdictions.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.prompt: The 'OPCW-The Hague Award' was recently conferred upon the Indian Chemical Council (ICC). Which of the following statements correctly describes the significance of this award?

options.text: It is the highest civilian honor given by the Netherlands to foreign industrial bodies for environmental protection.
It marks the first time a chemical industry body anywhere in the world has received this honor from the OPCW.
The award was given specifically for the ICC's role in the 26/11 Mumbai terror attack investigation.
It is a biennial award given to the National Authority of the country that destroys the most chemical stockpiles.

questions.explanation: In 2024, the Indian Chemical Council (ICC) was awarded the OPCW-The Hague Award. This was a significant milestone as it marked the first time a chemical industry body globally received this honor, acknowledging its contributions to safety, security, and the goals of the CWC.
questions.takeaway: The ICC is the first industry body globally to receive the OPCW-The Hague Award.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: In 2024, Indian Chemical Council (ICC) was awarded the OPCW-The Hague Award, marking the first time a chemical industry body anywhere in the world received this honour.
